mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in consumer price inflation captured by CPIAUCSL erode corporate real earnings expectations and elevate required equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns show positive Rank IC in conjunction with rising CPIAUCSL over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent signals substantial labor market slack that curtails wage income and consumer demand, leading to downward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment (UNRATE) combined with near-zero federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signals a liquidity trap environment where monetary stimulus fails to restore labor demand, leading markets to discount persistent earnings weakness and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE above 8 percent and FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent fails to produce negative Rank IC exceeding 0.03 on 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries, month-over-month acceleration in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals demand normalization that drives upward revenue surprises for consumer-facing firms and lifts aggregate S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 45 percent of observations during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) above 5 percent signal accelerating construction activity and downstream demand for materials and credit, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month HOUST increases exceeding 5 percent are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects rising manufacturing capacity utilization that translates into operating leverage and margin expansion for industrial and materials sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to exhibit a positive Rank IC above 0.03 on S&P 500 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals durable labor income expansion that supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth does not produce statistically significant positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth acceleration signals broad-based aggregate demand expansion that lifts top-line revenues across sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises fail to generate positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
